Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The model has a loop (E,P) meaning evaluate then optionally approve and loop back to evaluate? Actually loop in process tree: LOOP with children [E,P] means: execute E, then either exit or execute P then loop back to E.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So its a sequence of E then optionally P then repeat. That means evaluation can happen multiple times, and approval can happen multiple times, but they are in order: always E before each possible P.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: However, the model also introduces partial order anomalies: R->A, A->loop, loop->xor, and also A->C directly.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So C can happen after A, without waiting for loop or xor. That means closing can happen prematurely.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Also xor allows skip of N (notification).

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So anomalies: premature closure, skipping notification, repeated evaluation/approval cycles.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypotheses: business rule changes, miscommunication, technical errors, inadequate constraints.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Queries: use claim_events table to find claims where close activity occurs before evaluate or approve, or where multiple approves, or where no notification event.
